thesis: Dorchester Minerals is a passive U.S. royalty and mineral MLP that distributes essentially all available cash quarterly. FY2025 declared distributions were $2.775346 per unit on 48.26 million units; proof-first component math sums to about $19.24 per unit of economic value versus a price near $27.
why_market_wrong: Yield buyers may treat Dorchester as a static bond proxy and underweight 2024-2025 unit-funded mineral acquisitions and undeveloped acreage optionality. GAAP net income ($1.16/unit) understates cash distributions by design.
open_questions: Confirm live price and trailing distribution run-rate after Q1 2026 10-Q.
GP conflict and acquisition pacing vs dilution [HUMAN REVIEW].
Promote VIC PDF or activist context before weighting in base IRR.
